_provenance.note: This Stage 1 JSON was reconstructed from the currently-published cards so external Stage 2 (Gemini bias) and Stage 3 (Codex fact-check) reviews can verify what readers see. It is not the original analysis — the original pipeline was not run on this issue.

headline: Social Contract Concept Has No Single Agreed Definition
context: A 2025 academic review found that the 'social contract' (Malaysia's foundational bargain) has at least 7 competing definitions used by different political actors with contradictory implications.

cards.text: Everyone invokes the 'social contract.' Nobody agrees on what it is.
cards.sub: 7 competing definitions circulate in political discourse, from 'citizenship for loyalty' to 'equal partnership.'

cards.h: The phrase 'social contract' does not appear in the Federal Constitution.
cards.s: It is a political construct, powerful but undefined. Each side reads into it what it wishes to find.
cards.d: The phrase 'social contract' does not appear in the Federal Constitution. It is a political construct, powerful but undefined. Each side reads into it what it wishes to find.

cards.h: UMNO's version centres on Article 153 and Malay sovereignty.
cards.s: DAP's version centres on Article 8 and equal citizenship. Both claim the same 'social contract.'
cards.d: UMNO's version centres on Article 153 and Malay sovereignty. DAP's version centres on Article 8 and equal citizenship. Both claim the same 'social contract.'

cards.h: The Reid Commission's 1957 report describes a 'bargain' but not a 'contract.'
cards.s: The distinction matters: bargains can evolve. Contracts imply fixed, enforceable terms.
cards.d: The Reid Commission's 1957 report describes a 'bargain' but not a 'contract.' The distinction matters: bargains can evolve. Contracts imply fixed, enforceable terms.

cards.text: A foundational agreement that nobody defines the same way is not a foundation. It is a Rorschach test.
Malaysia needs to have the conversation it has avoided: what does the social contract mean in 2026? A concept read 7 different ways cannot govern a nation.

confidence: Low — derived from published cards, original brief not on disk; submit for retroactive Stage 2/3 verification
